compiled_state.title: Brainstorm Step 5 prior-art search targets sub-agent patterns instead of external integration domain when both signals fire together
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Bug Description

In brainstorm SKILL.md Step 5 (Prompt Alignment), when the epic involves both an `agent definitions` signal AND an external integration (e.g., Confluence), the prior-art search uses the first matched keyword in canonical order (`agent definitions`) as the search target. This causes the GitHub prior-art search to focus on sub-agent prompt patterns rather than the actual external integration being used.

## Observed Behavior

During brainstorm for the Confluence integration epic, Step 5 fired on both:
- `agent definitions` (new `dso:confluence-researcher` sub-agent)
- `skill file modifications` (changes to brainstorm/roadmap/implementation-plan SKILL.md)

The search query sent was: `site:github.com AI agent Confluence search sub-agent prompt research brainstorm context injection` — mixing sub-agent concerns with Confluence, producing low-signal results about generic context engineering rather than real Confluence API integration patterns.

## Expected Behavior

When the external integration epic signal (Step 2 web research trigger condition 1) overlaps with an LLM-instruction signal (Step 5), the prior-art search should prioritize the external integration domain. The search should be: `site:github.com Confluence CQL search content fetch authentication` — targeting the integration being built, not the agent architecture wrapping it.

The bot-psychologist dispatch is still warranted for reviewing the agent prompt itself, but the prior-art SEARCH should target the integration domain.

## Fix Direction

Step 5 should check whether the epic also triggered Step 2 condition 1 (external integration). When it did, the prior-art search should use the external integration name (e.g., Confluence, Stripe, GitHub) as the primary search subject rather than the matched LLM-instruction keyword category.
